component.name: Hikyaku
component.summary: Matrix chat client with activity awareness

description.p: Hikyaku is a Matrix chat client for GNOME built around activity awareness. It helps you stay on top of conversations by surfacing the rooms and direct messages that matter most, and lets an on-device AI assistant answer questions about Matrix and summarize room conversations — all without your data leaving your device.

ul.li: End-to-end encrypted messaging with key backup and recovery
Direct messages and group rooms with space organisation
On-device AI assistant powered by Ollama (optional)

description.p: Timeline architecture overhaul plus a swathe of jitter and reactions fixes.

ul.li: New Timeline GObject as the single chokepoint for per-room state: sort-by-timestamp, dedup-by-event-id, and index-in-sync invariants are now enforced at every mutation, eliminating an entire class of "reactions vanish, messages out of order, echoes duplicate" bugs.

Reactions now render live via a reactive notify handler — previously reactions arriving via sync could land on the message but never repaint the visible pill row if the row was off-screen or belonged to a cached non-current room.

Scroll jitter substantially reduced: bulk-splice tail eviction, single-splice room switch, coalesced async-markup drain that defers itself if scroll is still active, and a per-room per-row height estimate for pagination scroll restore.
Pagination is snappier: prefetch fires when scrolled within one viewport of the top instead of exactly at the top; the fetching-older cooldown dropped from 3000 ms to 300 ms.

Historical timeline loads correctly past the local-cache overlap window: the server-side pagination now loops with the running end token until it returns a chunk genuinely older than what the UI already has.
New-message tint applies on dedup-hit so the "unread while unfocused" highlight isn't silently dropped when bg_refresh pre-populates the timeline before the live sync event arrives.
Rust dependencies now build at opt-level 1 in debug (via .cargo/config.toml.example), removing a major contributor to release-build-only smoothness.
Peak memory reduced from ~4 GB to ~70 MB by sharing one EmojiChooser across all message rows instead of one per row, and by evicting cold per-room widget trees via an LRU cache.
Room entry and scroll: HTML→Pango conversion rewritten to byte-based scanning, cutting the worst per-message parse from ~38 ms to under 200 µs.
Smooth scrolling: GSK renderer pinned to Vulkan, removing per-frame offscreen fallback for GskTransformNode / GskMaskNode / GskContainerNode on affected drivers.
Room list: decoded avatar textures are now cached, so scrolling the sidebar no longer re-decodes images per row.
Reaction pills are pooled per row and reused across rebinds instead of being destroyed and recreated.
Added a scope-guard timing helper (src/perf.rs) and comprehensive instrumentation across the bind, room-entry, and focus-activation hot paths.
